images.caption: Figure 1:Performance (Pass@1, %) and training cost of Lightning OPD compared to standard OPD and the SFT baseline on Qwen3-4B-Base and Qwen3-8B-Base models. Lightning OPD matches or outperforms standard OPD on both math and coding benchmarks across both scales, while eliminating the need for a live teacher server during training. Notably, at the 8B scale, Lightning OPD achieves a state-of-the-art69.9%on AIME 2024 in just30 GPU hours, delivering4.0×\timeshigher training efficiency than standard OPD. These results highlight the effectiveness of Lightning OPD as a highly efficient post-training framework for large reasoning models.

Figure 2:Overview of Lightning OPD. In the SFT stage, the teacherπT\pi_{T}generates trajectories on𝒬SFT\mathcal{Q}_{\text{SFT}}then the base modelπbase\pi_{\text{base}}is fine-tuned on these trajectories to obtain the SFT modelπref\pi_{\text{ref}}. OPD stage proceeds in two phases. In the preprocessing phase, rollouts are sampled fromπref\pi_{\text{ref}}on𝒬OPD\mathcal{Q}_{\text{OPD}}and the teacher is queried once to compute and store per-token log-probabilitieslog⁡πT​(at∣st)\log\pi_{T}(a_{t}\mid s_{t}), forming the offline dataset𝒟OPD\mathcal{D}_{\text{OPD}}. In the training phase, the studentπθ\pi_{\theta}is initialized fromπref\pi_{\text{ref}}and trained on𝒟OPD\mathcal{D}_{\text{OPD}}. At each step, the per-token advantageAt=log⁡πT​(at∣st)−log⁡πθ​(at∣st)A_{t}=\log\pi_{T}(a_{t}\mid s_{t})-\log\pi_{\theta}(a_{t}\mid s_{t})is computed by readinglog⁡πT\log\pi_{T}from𝒟OPD\mathcal{D}_{\text{OPD}}while computinglog⁡πθ\log\pi_{\theta}online. During this process, we do not need a live teacher model. In contrast, standard OPD requires a live teacher server throughout training to compute advantages at every gradient step. By eliminating this requirement, Lightning OPD reduces total training cost of OPD by4.0×\timesat the 8B scale (from 120 to 30 GPU hours).
